bad_edge_localization.note: H1 distributes holonomy equally across the cycle. All cycle-A edges share the same obstruction; the corrupted edge is NOT uniquely identified within its cycle.

liar_vs_atom.explanation: Coherent liar = gauge shift: errors cancel in cycle sum -> zero holonomy -> H1 obstruction ~0. Incoherent atom = non-cancelling errors -> non-zero holonomy -> H1 obstruction > 0. This is the key discriminator.

h1_vs_ls.interpretation: H1 obstruction IS the LS edge residual; both are (I-BB^+)·residual. LS 'smears' the holonomy as node-value corrections across the graph; H1 names the same projection as a cohomological obstruction per edge. For gap DETECTION both are equivalent; H1 adds a structural frame.

threshold_baseline.h1_advantage: H1 detects cyclic holonomy STRUCTURALLY (via cycle-sum != 0), even when each individual edge error is sub-threshold. With threshold=0.08 and distributed error ~0.05/edge in a 3-cycle, threshold misses all edges but H1 reports holonomy = 0.15.

sub_threshold_holonomy_demo.interpretation: Each triangle-A edge error = 0.04 < threshold 0.08, so threshold misses all. Cycle holonomy = 0.12. H1 obstruction on each cycle-A edge = 0.04 (non-zero). H1 detects the holonomy that threshold cannot.

part_b.interpretation: NONE: no records carry both scalar-claim and relative-comparison language. H1 backbone does NOT apply to current AIOS memory as-is.
